title: Quarterly bus fares statistics: October to December 2024
body: " Quarterly bus fares user engagement  We would like to hear your views on the value and use of these statistics and whether this publication meets your needs. Any feedback provided will help inform the future design and development of this statistical release. Users can provide feedback by completing this short 5 minute survey or alternatively, you can email us.    Our statistical practice is regulated by the Office for Statistics Regulation (OSR). OSR sets the standards of trustworthiness, quality and value in the Code of Practice for Statistics that all producers of official statistics should adhere to. You are welcome to contact us directly by emailing us with any comments about how we meet these standards.   Statistics on local bus fares in Great Britain  The local bus fares index increased by 4.1% in England outside London between December 2023 and December 2024. The £2 fare cap for single tickets was still in place in December 2024, so this increase reflects changes to other fare products (such as season tickets).  For other areas, the local bus fares index change between December 2023 and December 2024 was:   1.2% increase in London 3.9% increase in metropolitan areas 4.3% increase in non-metropolitan areas 4.9% increase in Scotland 5.9% increase in Wales   The Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by 2.5% over the same 12-month period.  Contact us  Bus statistics     Email bus.statistics@dft.gov.uk     Media enquiries 0300 7777 878       To hear more about DfT statistical publications as they are released, follow us on X at DfTstats. "
